message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH       = process ALL 346 baskets from the outline (NOT one context window).
  FAITHFULNESS= context NLI entailment + STRICT numbers.

BINDING INVARIANTS (from docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md, non-negotiable):
- The faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI + numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate.
- No tool result renders directly. External content re-enters ONLY through the fold-in seam
  (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792).
- Computed numbers render ONLY through the verified lane (tradeoff_modeler ModelSpec / [#calc:]).
  Exploratory execute_python output is BARRED from rendering.
- WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never filter/cap. LAW VI: every knob env-tunable.

HARD RULES FOR YOU:
- Work ONLY inside undefined (branch undefined). NEVER touch another wheel's worktree.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never round up, never claim a pass you did not observe.
- If something fails, say it failed and paste the real lines.


YOU ARE THE INDEPENDENT GATE (Fable) for the POLARIS "undefined" wheel. Round 1.
You did NOT build this. A builder never grades its own homework. Be adversarial.

FOCUS OF THIS WHEEL: undefined

THE TESTER JUST RAN THE REAL CODE AND REPORTED:
{
  "ran": true,
  "command": "git -C /workspace/POLARIS worktree list; ls -d /home/polaris/polaris_project/undefined /workspace/POLARIS/undefined /workspace/undefined; grep -n \"wheel\\|worktree\\|branch\" /home/polaris/polaris_project/hamster_wheel.js; timeout 60 bash /home/polaris/polaris_project/deck.sh",
  "verdict": "BLOCKED",
  "real_output": "$ ls -d /home/polaris/polaris_project/undefined /workspace/POLARIS/undefined /workspace/undefined\nls: cannot access '/home/polaris/polaris_project/undefined': No such file or directory\nls: cannot access '/workspace/POLARIS/undefined': No such file or directory\nls: cannot access '/workspace/undefined': No such file or directory\n\n$ git -C /workspace/POLARIS worktree list\n/workspace/POLARIS                  8d682ec (detached HEAD)\n/home/polaris/wt/compete            0615bc5 [bot/compete-box]\n/home/polaris/wt/compose            0615bc5 [bot/compose-box]\n/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent      98726ca [bot/outline-agent-box]\n/home/polaris/wt/tooluse            98726ca [bot/outline-tooluse-box]\n/workspace/outline_agent_wt         ecda022 [bot/outline-agent]\n/workspace/outline_tooluse_wt       ecda022 [bot/outline-tooluse]\n/workspace/compose_wt               eff82fb [bot/sec-s5-compose-loop]\n(...18 worktrees total, NONE named 'undefined')\n\n$ grep -n hamster_wheel.js\n11:const W = args // {wheel, worktree, branch, focus, entrypoint_hint, progress_file}\n29:- Work ONLY inside ${W.worktree} (branch ${W.branch}). NEVER touch another wheel's worktree.\n113:log(`[${W.wheel}] hamster wheel starting — worktree ${W.worktree}, branch ${W.branch}`)\n129:YOU ARE THE TESTER for the POLARIS \"${W.wheel}\" wheel. Round ${round}.\n131:FOCUS OF THIS WHEEL: ${W.focus}\n136:1. cd ${W.worktree}. Read OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md and ${W.progress_file} (if it exists) for state.\n137:2. Find and RUN the real entrypoint for this wheel. ${W.entrypoint_hint}\n\n$ timeout 60 bash deck.sh\n=== POLARIS DECK  15:01:15 UTC ===\n\n-- wheels (workflow journals) --\n  wf_46688d62-abc    agents_done=0\n  wf_9947c8c5-332    agents_done=0\n  wf_dc886e1c-884    agents_done=0\n  wf_ef5d1757-3da    agents_done=0\n  wf_ef8097ef-46e    agents_done=0\n  wf_fb337142-607    agents_done=0\n  wf_fc6e4af2-fe1    agents_done=0\n\n-- commits (is anything actually landing?) --\n  outline_agent 98726ca 14:59 tooluse: WIP snapshot at wheel stop (unified into outline wheel)\n  tooluse       98726ca 14:59 tooluse: WIP snapshot at wheel stop (unified into outline wheel)\n  compose       0615bc5 14:23 compose: unblock the event loop — NLI verify off-loop via asyncio.to_thr\n\n-- SMOKING GUNS --\n  [429] OpenRouter rate-limit hits in 13 log(s) in last 10min — timings are DIRTY\n  [CONTENTION] root run_s5_i3.py (966951) still on OpenRouter -> sudo kill 817298 966951\n  [VRAM] orphan vLLM 8846 holding 31735 MiB -> sudo kill -9 8846\n  load=8.06  mem=62/2015GB  gpu=0 MiB 31735 MiB",
  "failures": [
    {
      "symptom": "Wheel launched with empty args payload: wheel/worktree/branch/focus/entrypoint_hint/progress_file all interpolated into the tester prompt as the literal string 'undefined'. There is no wheel to test.",
      "evidence": "hamster_wheel.js:11  const W = args // {wheel, worktree, branch, focus, entrypoint_hint, progress_file}",
      "suspected_component": "hamster_wheel.js arg passing (external launcher never populated `args`)"
    },
    {
      "symptom": "Target worktree does not exist — 'cd undefined' is unrunnable. No worktree or branch named 'undefined' exists anywhere on the box.",
      "evidence": "ls: cannot access '/home/polaris/polaris_project/undefined': No such file or directory",
      "suspected_component": "hamster_wheel.js:136 (`cd ${W.worktree}`)"
    },
    {
      "symptom": "Every `${W.*}` in the prompt template stringifies to 'undefined' rather than failing fast; the wheel happily spawns a tester with an incoherent, unexecutable task instead of erroring at startup.",
      "evidence": "hamster_wheel.js:113  log(`[${W.wheel}] hamster wheel starting — worktree ${W.worktree}, branch ${W.branch}`)",
      "suspected_component": "hamster_wheel.js — no arg validation before the round loop"
    },
    {
      "symptom": "OpenRouter HTTP 429 rate-limiting is live across 13 logs in the last 10 minutes; any timing measured this round is contended and untrustworthy.",
      "evidence": "[429] OpenRouter rate-limit hits in 13 log(s) in last 10min — timings are DIRTY",
      "suspected_component": "shared OpenRouter rate limit + unkillable root run_s5_i3.py (PID 966951)"
    }
  ],
  "blockers": [
    "HARD BLOCK: wheel args never passed. `const W = args` (hamster_wheel.js:11) received an empty/absent payload, so the tester prompt literally reads 'cd undefined', 'branch undefined', 'FOCUS OF THIS WHEEL: undefined'. No such worktree or branch exists (verified against `git worktree list` and `git branch -a`). Nothing to run.",
    "I deliberately did NOT guess a wheel. 3-4 wheels are running in parallel (deck.sh shows 7 live wf_* journals); picking a plausible worktree would violate the binding invariant 'NEVER touch another wheel's worktree' and could corrupt a live wheel's in-flight measurement. Clean BLOCKED beats a wrong guess.",
    "FIX: relaunch hamster_wheel.js with a populated args object. Real wheels per OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md + deck.sh: agentic-outline -> /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (bot/outline-agent-box); tool-use compute -> /home/polaris/wt/tooluse (bot/outline-tooluse-box); compose depth -> /home/polaris/wt/compose (bot/compose-box). Recommend adding a fail-fast arg check at the top of the round loop so a missing payload aborts instead of spawning 'undefined' testers.",
    "CAVEAT on the wheel list: outline_agent and tooluse now point at the SAME commit 98726ca ('tooluse: WIP snapshot at wheel stop (unified into outline wheel)'). Tooluse appears folded into the outline wheel — confirm before spawning both as separate wheels or two testers will collide on the same code.",
    "OpenRouter 429s ARE ACTIVE (13 logs in last 10 min) — reporting as instructed, did not retry. Root-owned run_s5_i3.py (PID 966951) is still hammering the same rate limit and is unkillable without sudo. Any compose timing taken now is dirty.",
    "Orphan root-owned vLLM EngineCore (PID 8846) holding 31,735 MiB VRAM on GPU1; needs `sudo kill -9 8846` to reclaim. Box driver runs as uid 1000 with no sudo."
  ],
  "metrics": {
    "undefined_worktrees_found": 0,
    "undefined_branches_found": 0,
    "total_worktrees_on_box": 18,
    "live_wheel_journals": 7,
    "wheel_journals_with_agents_done": 0,
    "logs_with_429_last_10min": 13,
    "orphan_vllm_vram_mib": 31735,
    "loadavg": 8.06,
    "heavy_runs_executed": 0,
    "real_wheel_entrypoints_run": 0
  }
}



DO THIS — and READ THE REAL CODE in undefined, do not reason from the summary alone:
1. LINE-BY-LINE READ of the real output above against the real source. Verify the tester's
   claims. Testers are wrong sometimes; a PASS that asserts nothing is a FAIL.
2. ROOT-CAUSE each failure to a MECHANISM at file:line. "It errors" is not a root cause.
   Severity: P0 = faithfulness breach (unsupported number rendered as verified, injection
   obeyed, silent wrong answer). P1 = wrong/unreachable result, undisclosed. P2 = cosmetic.
3. SOTA ASSESSMENT — the honest one. Does this wheel, AS IT ACTUALLY IS RIGHT NOW, beat
   ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on its axis? What is the concrete remaining gap?
   Do not flatter. If we are behind, say we are behind and by what.
4. FIX PLAN: concrete, file:line, front-loaded (most important first). Every step needs an
   ACCEPTANCE OBSERVABLE — the thing the next test run must SEE to prove the fix took.
5. SIGN_OFF: true ONLY if 0 P0 and 0 P1. NEVER force-approve. If the tester was BLOCKED and
   you cannot see real evidence, that is NOT a sign-off.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	export const meta = {
2	  name: 'hamster-wheel',
3	  description: 'POLARIS hamster wheel: Opus tests real output -> Fable root-causes + fix plan + SOTA verdict -> Opus builds -> Opus re-tests. Loops until Fable signs off.',
4	  phases: [
5	    { title: 'Test', detail: 'Opus runs the REAL entrypoint, reads output line-by-line' },
6	    { title: 'Fable gate', detail: 'Fresh Fable: root cause, fix plan, SOTA assessment, sign-off', model: 'fable' },
7	    { title: 'Build', detail: 'Opus applies the Fable plan, commits' },
8	  ],
9	}
10	
11	const W = args // {wheel, worktree, branch, focus, entrypoint_hint, progress_file}
12	const MAX_ROUNDS = 8
13	
14	// ---- shared context every subagent needs (they start fresh, so this must be self-contained)
15	const MISSION = `
16	POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
17	  DEPTH       = process ALL 346 baskets from the outline (NOT one context window).
18	  FAITHFULNESS= context NLI entailment + STRICT numbers.
19	
20	BINDING INVARIANTS (from docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md, non-negotiable):
21	- The faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI + numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate.
22	- No tool result renders directly. External content re-enters ONLY through the fold-in seam
23	  (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792).
24	- Computed numbers render ONLY through the verified lane (tradeoff_modeler ModelSpec / [#calc:]).
25	  Exploratory execute_python output is BARRED from rendering.
26	- WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never filter/cap. LAW VI: every knob env-tunable.
27	
28	HARD RULES FOR YOU:
29	- Work ONLY inside ${W.worktree} (branch ${W.branch}). NEVER touch another wheel's worktree.
30	- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never round up, never claim a pass you did not observe.
31	- If something fails, say it failed and paste the real lines.
32	`
33	
34	const TEST_SCHEMA = {
35	  type: 'object',
36	  required: ['ran', 'command', 'verdict', 'real_output', 'failures'],
37	  properties: {
38	    ran: { type: 'boolean', description: 'did a real command actually execute' },
39	    command: { type: 'string' },
40	    verdict: { enum: ['PASS', 'FAIL', 'BLOCKED', 'PARTIAL'] },
41	    real_output: { type: 'string', description: 'verbatim excerpt of the REAL output, incl. errors' },
42	    metrics: { type: 'object', description: 'measured numbers (timings, counts, pass/fail tallies)' },
43	    failures: {
44	      type: 'array',
45	      items: {
46	        type: 'object',
47	        required: ['symptom', 'evidence'],
48	        properties: {
49	          symptom: { type: 'string' },
50	          evidence: { type: 'string', description: 'file:line or verbatim output line' },
51	          suspected_component: { type: 'string' },
52	        },
53	      },
54	    },
55	    blockers: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
56	  },
57	}
58	
59	const PLAN_SCHEMA = {
60	  type: 'object',
61	  required: ['sign_off', 'sota', 'root_causes', 'fix_plan'],
62	  properties: {
63	    sign_off: { type: 'boolean', description: 'TRUE only if 0 P0 and 0 P1 remain. Never force-approve.' },
64	    sign_off_reason: { type: 'string' },
65	    sota: {
66	      type: 'object',
67	      required: ['at_sota', 'gap'],
68	      properties: {
69	        at_sota: { type: 'boolean', description: 'does this wheel genuinely beat ChatGPT/Gemini/FS-Researcher on its axis' },
70	        gap: { type: 'string', description: 'the concrete remaining gap to SOTA, or why we are already past it' },
71	      },
72	    },
73	    root_causes: {
74	      type: 'array',
75	      items: {
76	        type: 'object',
77	        required: ['severity', 'file', 'why'],
78	        properties: {
79	          severity: { enum: ['P0', 'P1', 'P2'] },
80	          file: { type: 'string', description: 'file:line' },
81	          why: { type: 'string', description: 'the actual mechanism, not a symptom restatement' },
82	        },
83	      },
84	    },
85	    fix_plan: {
86	      type: 'array',
87	      items: {
88	        type: 'object',
89	        required: ['step', 'file', 'change', 'acceptance'],
90	        properties: {
91	          step: { type: 'integer' },
92	          file: { type: 'string' },
93	          change: { type: 'string', description: 'concrete, file:line specific' },
94	          acceptance: { type: 'string', description: 'the OBSERVABLE that proves it worked' },
95	        },
96	      },
97	    },
98	  },
99	}
100	
101	const BUILD_SCHEMA = {
102	  type: 'object',
103	  required: ['applied', 'commit_sha', 'honest_status'],
104	  properties: {
105	    applied: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
106	    skipped: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'plan steps NOT done, and why' },
107	    commit_sha: { type: 'string' },
108	    files_changed: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
109	    honest_status: { type: 'string', description: 'what actually landed vs what the plan asked for' },
110	  },
111	}
112	
113	log(`[${W.wheel}] hamster wheel starting — worktree ${W.worktree}, branch ${W.branch}`)
114	
115	const history = []
116	let signedOff = false
117	
118	for (let round = 1; round <= MAX_ROUNDS; round++) {
119	  if (budget.total && budget.remaining() < 60_000) {
120	    log(`[${W.wheel}] stopping: token budget nearly spent`)
121	    break
122	  }
123	
124	  // ---------- 1. TEST: run the real thing, read the real output ----------
125	  phase('Test')
126	  const test = await agent(
127	    `${MISSION}
128	
129	YOU ARE THE TESTER for the POLARIS "${W.wheel}" wheel. Round ${round}.
130	
131	FOCUS OF THIS WHEEL: ${W.focus}
132	
133	${round > 1 ? `PREVIOUS ROUND: the builder applied a fix. Here is what it claims it did:\n${JSON.stringify(history[history.length - 1]?.build ?? {}, null, 2)}\n\nYour job is to find out whether that is TRUE by running the code.` : ''}
134	
135	DO THIS:
136	1. cd ${W.worktree}. Read OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md and ${W.progress_file} (if it exists) for state.
137	2. Find and RUN the real entrypoint for this wheel. ${W.entrypoint_hint}
138	   - Run it FOREGROUND with a HARD timeout (e.g. \`timeout 900 python ...\`). NEVER nohup+tail -f.
139	   - Prefer the cheapest run that still exercises the REAL path. A unit test that mocks the
140	     thing under test proves nothing — drive the actual code.
141	3. READ THE OUTPUT LINE BY LINE. Do not skim. Do not trust a green checkmark; check that the
142	   assertion actually asserted something real.
143	4. Report VERBATIM output, including failures. If it is blocked, say BLOCKED and why.
144	
145	RESOURCE DISCIPLINE: one heavy run at a time. Other wheels are running in parallel and share
146	the OpenRouter rate limit — if you see HTTP 429, SAY SO in blockers, do not silently retry forever.`,
147	    { label: `${W.wheel}:test:r${round}`, phase: 'Test', schema: TEST_SCHEMA },
148	  )
149	
150	  if (!test) {
151	    log(`[${W.wheel}] r${round}: tester died, aborting wheel`)
152	    break
153	  }
154	  log(`[${W.wheel}] r${round} TEST -> ${test.verdict} (${test.failures?.length ?? 0} failures)`)
155	
156	  // ---------- 2. FABLE GATE: root cause + fix plan + SOTA verdict ----------
157	  phase('Fable gate')
158	  const plan = await agent(
159	    `${MISSION}
160	
161	YOU ARE THE INDEPENDENT GATE (Fable) for the POLARIS "${W.wheel}" wheel. Round ${round}.
162	You did NOT build this. A builder never grades its own homework. Be adversarial.
163	
164	FOCUS OF THIS WHEEL: ${W.focus}
165	
166	THE TESTER JUST RAN THE REAL CODE AND REPORTED:
167	${JSON.stringify(test, null, 2)}
168	
169	${history.length ? `PRIOR ROUNDS (what was tried, and whether it worked):\n${JSON.stringify(history.map(h => ({ round: h.round, verdict: h.test.verdict, signed: h.plan?.sign_off, built: h.build?.honest_status })), null, 2)}` : ''}
170	
171	DO THIS — and READ THE REAL CODE in ${W.worktree}, do not reason from the summary alone:
172	1. LINE-BY-LINE READ of the real output above against the real source. Verify the tester's
173	   claims. Testers are wrong sometimes; a PASS that asserts nothing is a FAIL.
174	2. ROOT-CAUSE each failure to a MECHANISM at file:line. "It errors" is not a root cause.
175	   Severity: P0 = faithfulness breach (unsupported number rendered as verified, injection
176	   obeyed, silent wrong answer). P1 = wrong/unreachable result, undisclosed. P2 = cosmetic.
177	3. SOTA ASSESSMENT — the honest one. Does this wheel, AS IT ACTUALLY IS RIGHT NOW, beat
178	   ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on its axis? What is the concrete remaining gap?
179	   Do not flatter. If we are behind, say we are behind and by what.
180	4. FIX PLAN: concrete, file:line, front-loaded (most important first). Every step needs an
181	   ACCEPTANCE OBSERVABLE — the thing the next test run must SEE to prove the fix took.
182	5. SIGN_OFF: true ONLY if 0 P0 and 0 P1. NEVER force-approve. If the tester was BLOCKED and
183	   you cannot see real evidence, that is NOT a sign-off.`,
184	    { label: `${W.wheel}:fable-gate:r${round}`, phase: 'Fable gate', model: 'fable', schema: PLAN_SCHEMA, effort: 'high' },
185	  )
186	
187	  if (!plan) {
188	    log(`[${W.wheel}] r${round}: Fable gate died, aborting wheel`)
189	    break
190	  }
191	
192	  const p0p1 = (plan.root_causes ?? []).filter(r => r.severity === 'P0' || r.severity === 'P1').length
193	  log(`[${W.wheel}] r${round} FABLE -> sign_off=${plan.sign_off} | P0/P1=${p0p1} | SOTA=${plan.sota?.at_sota} | ${plan.sota?.gap?.slice(0, 90) ?? ''}`)
194	
195	  if (plan.sign_off) {
196	    history.push({ round, test, plan, build: null })
197	    signedOff = true
198	    log(`[${W.wheel}] *** SIGNED OFF at round ${round} ***`)
199	    break
200	  }
201	
202	  // ---------- 3. BUILD: apply the Fable plan ----------
203	  phase('Build')
204	  const build = await agent(
205	    `${MISSION}
206	
207	YOU ARE THE BUILDER for the POLARIS "${W.wheel}" wheel. Round ${round}.
208	
209	The independent Fable gate REJECTED the current state and produced this plan.
210	Execute it. Do not freelance; if you disagree with a step, do it anyway OR skip it and say why
211	in "skipped" — never silently deviate.
212	
213	FABLE'S ROOT CAUSES + FIX PLAN:
214	${JSON.stringify({ root_causes: plan.root_causes, fix_plan: plan.fix_plan, sota: plan.sota }, null, 2)}
215	
216	THE REAL TEST OUTPUT THAT TRIGGERED THIS:
217	${test.real_output?.slice(0, 4000) ?? '(none)'}
218	
219	DO THIS:
220	1. cd ${W.worktree}. Apply the fix plan, top-down (it is front-loaded).
221	2. EXERCISE EVERY PATH YOU TOUCH before committing. py_compile is NOT enough — the last driver
222	   shipped a fix whose error path would have TypeError'd at runtime because it only compiled it.
223	   Actually call the function on the failure path it handles.
224	3. git commit in ${W.worktree} with an HONEST message. If the fix is partial, the commit message
225	   must say PARTIAL and what is still open.
226	4. Append a dated one-line status to ${W.progress_file}.
227	5. Report honestly what landed vs what the plan asked for. A skipped step reported is fine.
228	   A skipped step hidden is not.`,
229	    { label: `${W.wheel}:build:r${round}`, phase: 'Build', schema: BUILD_SCHEMA },
230	  )
231	
232	  if (!build) {
233	    log(`[${W.wheel}] r${round}: builder died, aborting wheel`)
234	    history.push({ round, test, plan, build: null })
235	    break
236	  }
237	  log(`[${W.wheel}] r${round} BUILD -> ${build.commit_sha ?? 'no commit'} | ${build.honest_status?.slice(0, 100) ?? ''}`)
238	
239	  history.push({ round, test, plan, build })
240	}
241	
242	const last = history[history.length - 1]
243	log(`[${W.wheel}] wheel done — ${history.length} round(s), signed_off=${signedOff}`)
244	
245	return {
246	  wheel: W.wheel,
247	  worktree: W.worktree,
248	  branch: W.branch,
249	  signed_off: signedOff,
250	  rounds: history.length,
251	  final_sota: last?.plan?.sota ?? null,
252	  final_verdict: last?.test?.verdict ?? null,
253	  open_p0p1: (last?.plan?.root_causes ?? []).filter(r => r.severity !== 'P2'),
254	  timeline: history.map(h => ({
255	    round: h.round,
256	    test: h.test?.verdict,
257	    metrics: h.test?.metrics,
258	    sign_off: h.plan?.sign_off,
259	    at_sota: h.plan?.sota?.at_sota,
260	    gap: h.plan?.sota?.gap,
261	    commit: h.build?.commit_sha,
262	    landed: h.build?.honest_status,
263	  })),
264	}
265

input.description: Locate workflow journal files referenced by deck.sh

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# POLARIS master monitoring deck. Read-only. Run any time: bash deck.sh
3	WFDIR=/home/polaris/.claude/projects/-home-polaris-polaris-project/ea997c8e-37cf-4d9a-8a80-f7728137f18b/subagents/workflows
4	
5	printf '\033[1m=== POLARIS DECK  %s UTC ===\033[0m\n' "$(date -u +%H:%M:%S)"
6	
7	printf '\n\033[1m-- wheels (workflow journals) --\033[0m\n'
8	for wf in "$WFDIR"/wf_*; do
9	  [ -d "$wf" ] || continue
10	  j="$wf/journal.jsonl"
11	  [ -f "$j" ] || { printf '  %-18s (no journal yet)\n' "$(basename "$wf")"; continue; }
12	  # last log line tells us the round + phase
13	  last=$(grep -o '"message":"[^"]*"' "$j" 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | sed 's/"message":"//;s/"$//')
14	  n=$(grep -c '"type":"agent_end"' "$j" 2>/dev/null); n=${n:-0}
15	  printf '  %-18s agents_done=%-3s %s\n' "$(basename "$wf" | cut -c1-18)" "$n" "${last:0:95}"
16	done
17	
18	printf '\n\033[1m-- commits (is anything actually landing?) --\033[0m\n'
19	for d in outline_agent tooluse compose; do
20	  wt=/home/polaris/wt/$d
21	  [ -d "$wt" ] || continue
22	  printf '  %-13s %s\n' "$d" "$(git -C "$wt" log -1 --pretty='%h %ad %s' --date=format:'%H:%M' 2>/dev/null | cut -c1-88)"
23	  dirty=$(git -C "$wt" status --porcelain | wc -l)
24	  [ "$dirty" -gt 0 ] && printf '                \033[33m%s file(s) uncommitted\033[0m\n' "$dirty"
25	done
26	
27	printf '\n\033[1m-- SMOKING GUNS --\033[0m\n'
28	hit=0
29	
30	# 1. OpenRouter 429 contention — the #1 measurement poisoner
31	r429=$(find /home/polaris/wt /workspace/POLARIS/logs -name '*.log' -mmin -10 2>/dev/null \
32	       | xargs grep -lc '429' 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
33	if [ "$r429" -gt 0 ]; then
34	  printf '  \033[31m[429]\033[0m OpenRouter rate-limit hits in %s log(s) in last 10min — timings are DIRTY\n' "$r429"; hit=1
35	fi
36	
37	# 2. The root job that poisons every measurement
38	if ps -p 966951 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
39	  printf '  \033[31m[CONTENTION]\033[0m root run_s5_i3.py (966951) still on OpenRouter -> sudo kill 817298 966951\n'; hit=1
40	fi
41	
42	# 3. Leaked VRAM
43	if ps -p 8846 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
44	  printf '  \033[33m[VRAM]\033[0m orphan vLLM 8846 holding %s MiB -> sudo kill -9 8846\n' \
45	    "$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used --format=csv,noheader,nounits | sed -n 2p)"; hit=1
46	fi
47	
48	# 4. A wheel that claims a pass but committed nothing
49	# 5. Resource blowout
50	load=$(cut -d' ' -f1 /proc/loadavg)
51	mem=$(free -g | awk '/^Mem:/{printf "%d/%dGB", $3, $2}')
52	printf '  load=%s  mem=%s  gpu=%s\n' "$load" "$mem" "$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used --format=csv,noheader | paste -sd' ')"
53	[ "$hit" -eq 0 ] && printf '  \033[32mno new smoking guns\033[0m\n'
54	exit 0
55

Check driver prompt and launcher for wheel invocation

content.content:
9:WHEELS (each in its OWN worktree, NEVER two on one):
14:COORDINATION (avoid clobber): the laptop session may still be finishing an agentic-outline iteration for a minute. START with the compose parallel-depth wheel and the tool-use wheel. Pick up the agentic-outline wheel only after you confirm /workspace/outline_agent_wt has no other active editor (git status clean + no .py mtime churn in the last 2 min).
16:LOOP per wheel: run on the box (foreground, HARD timeout, NEVER nohup+tail -f) -> fresh Fable judge reads the REAL output line-by-line -> SIGN_OFF (0 P0/P1) or a comprehensive front-loaded fix list -> hands apply -> re-run. Never force-approve. Keep GPU/RAM sane: one heavy run at a time per subsystem, kill leaked contexts.
18:Work CONTINUOUSLY. When a wheel signs off, chain the next / re-run downstream. Do NOT stop until 15:00 UTC or all wheels beat both competitors. Every ~15 min append a one-line status to OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md and git commit.
===
#!/bin/bash
export HOME=/home/polaris
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project || exit 1
i=0
while true; do
  i=$((i+1))
  echo "=== driver start #$i $(date -u) ===" >> driver.log
  s=$(date +%s)
  claude -p "$(cat DRIVER_PROMPT.txt)" --dangerously-skip-permissions --verbose >> driver.log 2>&1
  e=$(date +%s)
  echo "=== driver exited #$i after $((e-s))s $(date -u) ===" >> driver.log
  if [ $((e-s)) -lt 60 ]; then sleep 120; else sleep 15; fi
done
